name: 重庆
description: Chongqing, China's largest city by area, is a mountainous metropolis built on cliffs along the Yangtze and Jialing Rivers. Known as the 'Mountain City' and 'Fog City' for its dramatic terrain and misty climate. Birthplace of Sichuan cuisine and hot pot. A strategic inland port and commercial hub with a unique food culture.
highlights: Dazu Rock Carvings - UNESCO World Heritage

climate.tips: Summers are extremely hot and humid (40C possible). Winters are damp and cold. Best in spring and autumn. Always carry water in summer. Fog is common year-round.

attractions.name: 解放碑
attractions.description: Chongqing's commercial center and iconic landmark, a bustling pedestrian area with shopping, dining, and entertainment.
attractions.tips: Best at night when illuminated. Sample street food from vendors. Walk to nearby Monggan Street.

attractions.name: 洪崖洞
attractions.description: Stunning cliff-side stilted house complex with traditional architecture, famous for appearing like a 'castle in the sky' at night.
attractions.tips: Visit at dusk for photos of both day and night views. Crowds peak 19:00-21:00. Best photos from大桥 or river boat.

attractions.name: 磁器口古镇
attractions.description: Historic millstream town dating back to Ming Dynasty, famous for traditional crafts, especially pottery and embroidered cloth.
attractions.tips: Weekday mornings for fewer crowds. Try Chongqing noodles and Ma yogurt. Avoid peak holidays.

attractions.name: 南山一棵树观景台
attractions.description: Premier night viewing spot overlooking the city's spectacular skyline, particularly beautiful where Yangtze and Jialing Rivers meet.
attractions.tips: Go at sunset for day and night views. Bring tripod for photos. Taxi from downtown about 30¥.

attractions.name: 大足石刻
attractions.description: UNESCO World Heritage site with extraordinary Buddhist and Taoist rock carvings dating from 9th to 13th century.
attractions.tips: Arrange day trip or stay nearby. Start early to avoid tour groups. Wear comfortable walking shoes.

attractions.name: 武隆喀斯特
attractions.description: UNESCO World Heritage site featuring dramatic karst formations, massive caves, and natural bridges in stunning natural setting.
attractions.tips: Day trip from Chongqing possible but early start needed. Bring jacket - caves are cold. Film location for 'Curse of the Golden Flower'.

attractions.name: 十八梯
attractions.description: Restored historic neighborhood connecting upper and lower Chongqing with traditional architecture and local atmosphere.
attractions.tips: Best early morning. Combine with Jiefangbei walk. Great for photos of old Chongqing.

attractions.name: 白帝城
attractions.description: Ancient city on the Yangtze featuring the famous 'Three Gorges' scenery and associated with legendary King of Spring and Autumn.
attractions.tips: Combine with Three Gorges cruise. Named after Liu Bei's 'White Emperor' legend.

attractions.name: 长江索道
attractions.description: Iconic cable car system crossing the Yangtze River, offering spectacular views of the city skyline and river confluence.
attractions.tips: Go early morning or late evening to avoid crowds. Best during sunset for day/night views.

attractions.name: 李子坝轻轨穿楼
attractions.description: World-famous metro station built through a residential building, a unique example of Chongqing's innovative urban design.
attractions.tips: Best viewed from the dedicated viewing platform across the street. Ride the metro through the building for the full experience.

attractions.name: 鹅岭公园
attractions.description: One of Chongqing's oldest and most beautiful parks, featuring traditional Chinese gardens, tea houses, and panoramic city views.
attractions.tips: Visit at sunrise for peaceful atmosphere. The park connects to Jiaotong University campus.

attractions.name: 渣子洞
attractions.description: Former prison where revolutionary prisoners were held during the 1940s, now a museum honoring those who fought for freedom.
attractions.tips: Combine with White Manor nearby. Allow 2 hours for both sites. Book in advance during peak season.

attractions.name: 白公馆
attractions.description: Historic villa turned prison during the 1940s, site of significant events in Chinese revolutionary history.
attractions.tips: Part of the Red Cliff scenic area with Zhazidong. Morning recommended to avoid afternoon crowds.

attractions.name: 重庆动物园
attractions.description: Large zoological park home to giant pandas, golden monkeys, and over 300 species of animals in natural habitats.
attractions.tips: Arrive early at 08:00 to see pandas when most active. Giant panda exhibition fee separate.

attractions.name: 人民公园
attractions.description: Urban park in the heart of the city with tea houses, gardens, and historical monuments, popular with locals.
attractions.tips: Best in early morning to see locals practicing tai chi. Traditional tea service available.

attractions.name: 重庆园博园
attractions.description: Massive botanical garden featuring regional gardens from across China and international themes with beautiful landscapes.
attractions.tips: Rent a golf cart for easier navigation. Spring for flower exhibitions. Take metro Line 3.

attractions.name: 重庆欢乐谷
attractions.description: Large amusement park with thrilling rides, water park, and entertainment shows for all ages.
attractions.tips: Weekdays less crowded. Summer for water park. Express passes available. Lockers for bags.

attractions.name: 海昌加勒比水世界
attractions.description: Large water park with Caribbean-themed rides, wave pools, and slides for summer fun.
attractions.tips: Go early to avoid lines. Bring sunscreen. Lockers required. Food inside expensive.

attractions.name: 重庆科技馆
attractions.description: Modern science museum with interactive exhibits on physics, technology, and natural history.
attractions.tips: Best for families with children. Book IMAX tickets online. Allow half day.

attractions.name: 三峡博物馆
attractions.description: National museum showcasing the history and culture of the Three Gorges region and ancient Ba-Yu civilization.
attractions.tips: Combine with People's Park and People's Hall nearby. Audio guides available. Photography restricted.

attractions.name: 武隆芙蓉洞
attractions.description: One of the largest and most beautiful karst caves in China, featuring spectacular stalactites, stalagmites, and underground rivers.
attractions.tips: Bring a light jacket - cave temperature is cool. Photography allowed but no flash. Combine with Three Natural Bridges visit.

attractions.name: 福盛茶馆
attractions.description: Traditional Chongqing teahouse dating back to Qing Dynasty, offering authentic tea service and mahjong in historic setting.
attractions.tips: Best experienced in the morning. Try local Sichuan tea. Join locals for mahjong if you know the game. Budget for tipping.

attractions.name: 长寿湖
attractions.description: Large freshwater lake known for its beautiful sunset views, island-hopping, and fresh fish cuisine. About 2 hours from Chongqing.
attractions.tips: Best visited as a weekend getaway. Take a boat tour around the islands. Try the famous Changshou lake fish hot pot. Stay overnight at lakeside hotel.

attractions.name: 黑石板
attractions.description: Historic black stone board street with traditional Chongqing architecture, local snacks, and cultural heritage.
attractions.tips: Best in early morning. Combine with Jiefangbei walk. Try local breakfast. Great for photography.

attractions.name: 观音桥步行街
attractions.description: Modern commercial district in Jiangbei with shopping centers, restaurants, and entertainment. Chongqing's second major business district.
attractions.tips: Great for evening strolls. Try the night food street. Metro Line 3 directly connects. Modern alternative to Jiefangbei.

attractions.name: 铜锣峡
attractions.description: Scenic gorge on the Yangtze River featuring dramatic cliffs, ancient temples, and natural beauty. Historic military defense point.
attractions.tips: Best combined with cruise on Yangtze. Ancient military fortifications visible. Bring hiking shoes. Cooler temperature by river.

restaurants.name: 珮姐老火锅
restaurants.description: Legendary hot pot chain with original family recipe. Known for authentic Chongqing numbing-spicy broth.

restaurants.name: 周师兄大刀腰片
restaurants.nameEn: Zhou Shixiong Giant Knife Kidney Slices
restaurants.description: Famous for signature kidney slices cut paper-thin and hot pot with traditional broth.

restaurants.name: 小天鹅火锅
restaurants.description: One-diamond Black Pearl hot pot restaurant, pioneer of modern Chongqing hot pot chain.

restaurants.name: 重庆小面
restaurants.description: Essential Chongqing breakfast - spicy noodles with peanut powder, pickles, and Sichuan pepper.

restaurants.name: 降龙爪爪
restaurants.description: Popular late-night snack spot famous for tender braised chicken claws in various flavors.

restaurants.name: 李子坝梁山鸡
restaurants.description: Famous for cilantro-heavy chicken hot pot, a unique Chongqing specialty near the metro station.

restaurants.name: 好又来酸辣粉
restaurants.nameEn: Hao You Lai Sour Spicy Noodles
restaurants.description: Iconic sour and spicy potato noodles, a beloved Chongqing street food.

restaurants.name: 老码头火锅
restaurants.description: Traditional Chongqing hot pot in river view setting. Authentic local favorite.

restaurants.name: 石灰寺火锅
restaurants.description: Popular local hot pot restaurant near 18梯, known for authentic broth and fresh ingredients.

restaurants.name: 枇杷园火锅
restaurants.description: Famous hillside hot pot restaurant with stunning views of the city lights at night.

restaurants.name: 秦妈火锅
restaurants.description: Well-known hot pot chain with consistent quality and many branch locations.

restaurants.name: 大龙火锅
restaurants.description: Legendary late-night hot pot spot popular with students and night owls.

restaurants.name: 刘一手火锅
restaurants.description: Popular hot pot chain offering reliable Chongqing-style hot pot with various broth options.

restaurants.name: 遇见老小面
restaurants.description: Popular breakfast spot famous for authentic Chongqing xiao mian with perfect spice balance.

restaurants.name: 张记面庄
restaurants.description: Local favorite for Chongqing xiao mian with rich, numbing-spicy flavor profile.

restaurants.name: 梯坎面
restaurants.description: Famous for noodles served on the steps, a unique Chongqing dining experience.

restaurants.name: 花市豌杂面
restaurants.description: Legendary noodle shop with creamy pea sauce noodles, a Chongqing specialty not to miss.

restaurants.name: 九园包子
restaurants.description: Historic Chongqing breakfast spot famous for steamed buns with various fillings.

restaurants.name: 吴抄手
restaurants.description: Famous for hand-made dumplings with Chongqing-style spicy dipping sauce.

restaurants.name: 过道豆花
restaurants.description: Popular tofu pudding restaurant with savory and sweet options, a local comfort food favorite.

arrival.tips: Airport 25km from city center. Metro Line 3 to downtown. Airport bus to Jiefangbei.
High-speed rail from Chengdu very convenient. Chongqing North is near business district.

metro: Note: Many stations have exits on different levels
bus: Easiest to hail Didi for direct routes
Yangtze Cruise: Three Gorges cruises depart from Chongqing docks
Book through travel agency or online
bike: City bike stations near tourist areas

hotels.name: 重庆来福士洲际酒店
hotels.bookingTips: Book river view rooms. The building itself is architectural marvel.

hotels.name: 重庆解放碑威斯汀酒店
hotels.bookingTips: Best location for shopping and food. Weekend rates lower.

hotels.name: 重庆希尔顿酒店
hotels.bookingTips: Book river view. Good for first-time visitors.

hotels.name: 重庆南方君临酒店
hotels.bookingTips: Better rates on weekdays. Ask for river view room.

hotels.name: 重庆长江黄金游轮
hotels.bookingTips: Book 3+ months ahead for peak season. Window cabins worth premium.

hotels.name: 重庆JW万豪酒店
hotels.bookingTips: Best location for tourists. Book club rooms for better service.

hotels.name: 重庆喜来登酒店
hotels.bookingTips: Best river view rooms. Walking distance to attractions.

hotels.name: 重庆解放碑美居酒店
hotels.bookingTips: Best budget option near Jiefangbei. Book direct.

hotels.name: 重庆万豪酒店
hotels.bookingTips: Good for both business and leisure. Weekend rates available.

hotels.name: 重庆雅诗特酒店
hotels.bookingTips: Book river view rooms. Great for night photography.

hotels.name: 重庆国贸格兰维酒店
hotels.bookingTips: Combine with Three Gorges Museum visit. Good breakfast.

hotels.name: 重庆丽笙世嘉酒店
hotels.bookingTips: Best for night views. Book high floor rooms.

hotels.name: 重庆万达文华酒店
hotels.bookingTips: Book club rooms. Weekend rates available.

hotels.name: 重庆典雅戴斯酒店
hotels.bookingTips: Best budget near Jiefangbei. Book early for discounts.

hotels.name: 重庆雾都宾馆
hotels.bookingTips: Experience local culture. Good for first-time visitors.

tips: Works at all hot pot restaurants

culturalTips.title: Spice Tolerance
culturalTips.content: Chongqing cuisine is extremely spicy. Start with '微辣' (mild) if unsure. Sichuan pepper creates 'numbing' sensation - it's pleasant, not unpleasant.

culturalTips.title: Mountain Terrain
culturalTips.content: Chongqing is built on steep hills. Prepare for lots of stairs and walking uphill. Metro stations have multiple levels. Wear comfortable shoes.

culturalTips.title: Fog City
culturalTips.content: Chongqing is called 'Fog City' with frequent mist. Views can be limited. Best visibility in late morning. Nanshan platform best on clearer days.

culturalTips.title: Hot Pot Culture
culturalTips.content: Hot pot is Chongqing's signature. 'Gongbao' and 'MaLa' are the main styles. Dip ingredients in egg yolk for tenderness. Try the dipping sauce combinations locals use.

culturalTips.title: Pier Culture
culturalTips.content: Chongqing's location at the confluence of Yangtze and Jialing Rivers gave rise to a rich pier culture. '码头工人' (pier workers) shaped the city's work ethic. Many local dishes originated from food served at piers. The 'Lao Ma Tou' (Old Pier) areas preserve this heritage.

culturalTips.title: Chili and Sichuan Pepper Culture
culturalTips.content: Chongqing cuisine features two distinctive sensations: '辣' (spicy/hot from chili) and '麻' (numbing from Sichuan pepper). The combination '麻辣' defines the flavor profile. Locals believe it helps combat the humid climate and stimulates appetite in summer.

culturalTips.title: Fog and Damp Climate
culturalTips.content: Chongqing is called 'Fog City' (雾都) due to its humid climate. The fog is most prevalent in spring and winter. Locals adapted by eating spicy food to ward off dampness and by building homes on hills for better ventilation.

culturalTips.title: Life on Steep Terrain
culturalTips.content: The mountain terrain means locals use more stairs than anywhere else in China. Buildings have multiple levels connected by escalators. The phrase '爬坡上坎' (climbing slopes and crossing steps) describes daily life. Wear comfortable shoes.

culturalTips.title: Night Food Culture
culturalTips.content: Chongqing has vibrant night food culture. Hot pot restaurants stay open until 2-3 AM. Street vendors and night markets peak after 9 PM. '深夜食堂' (late-night canteens) are an essential part of social life.

culturalTips.title: Traditional Tea House Culture
culturalTips.content: Traditional tea houses (茶馆) are social hubs where locals drink tea, play mahjong, and socialize. '喝茶' (drinking tea) is a daily ritual. Popular types include '碧螺春' and '普洱'. Tea houses in People's Park and Eling Park are authentic experiences.

culturalTips.title: Sichuan Dialect
culturalTips.content: The Chongqing dialect (重庆话) is part of the Sichuanese Mandarin family. It's known for being more direct and forceful than Beijing Mandarin. '要得' (yes), '哈子' (foolish), and '雄起' (go for it) are common expressions.

culturalTips.title: River Commerce Heritage
culturalTips.content: Historically, Chongqing was a major river port for the Three Gorges trade. The city's wealth and architecture reflect its commercial past. '棒棒军' (porter groups) still exist, carrying goods up the steep streets.

culturalTips.title: Extreme Summer Heat
culturalTips.content: Chongqing is one of China's ' furnaces' (火炉城市). Summer temperatures regularly exceed 40C with high humidity. Locals escape to air-conditioned malls and night markets. Drink plenty of water and take afternoon breaks.

culturalTips.title: Wartime Capital Heritage
culturalTips.content: During WWII, Chongqing served as China's wartime capital (陪都). Many buildings, museums, and historical sites preserve this period. The city was heavily bombed but rebuilt. '抗战遗址' (war heritage sites) are important cultural landmarks.

culturalTips.title: Mahjong Social Culture
culturalTips.content: Mahjong (麻将) is the quintessential Chongqing social activity. Locals play in tea houses, homes, and dedicated parlors. Games often last hours with betting. '血战到底' (Fight to the End) is the popular local rule variant.

culturalTips.title: Bamboo Carrier Tradition
culturalTips.content: The '棒棒军' (bamboo carrier army) emerged from Chongqing's hilly terrain. Carriers use bamboo poles to carry goods up and down steep streets. While less common today, they remain a symbol of Chongqing's working-class spirit.

culturalTips.title: Chongqing Crosstalk
culturalTips.content: Chongqing has a vibrant traditional comedy scene with '相声' (crosstalk) performances. Local dialect comedians perform in teahouses and theaters. It's an excellent way to experience local humor and language.

culturalTips.title: Ancient Ba-Yu Culture
culturalTips.content: Chongqing is the heart of ancient Ba-Yu culture (巴渝文化), one of China's oldest regional cultures dating back 3,000 years. The Ba people were known for their boat-making, stone carving, and distinctive folk songs. Many traditions survive in local customs.

culturalTips.title: Stilted House Architecture
culturalTips.content: Chongqing's iconic stilted houses (吊脚楼) were built on cliffs and riverbanks to maximize space on hilly terrain. Hongyadong recreates this traditional architecture. The houses feature wooden structures with multiple levels.

culturalTips.title: River Boat Culture
culturalTips.content: Boats have been central to Chongqing life for centuries. '川江号子' (Sichuan river songs) were work songs chanted by boatmen. Yangtze cruises and river ferries continue this tradition. Evening boat tours offer beautiful city views.

culturalTips.title: Bridge Engineering Marvel
culturalTips.content: Chongqing has more bridges than any other Chinese city due to its river and mountain terrain. The Egongyan Bridge and Caiyuanba Bridge are engineering wonders. The cable car and light rail through buildings showcase innovative urban design.

culturalTips.title: Giant Panda Connection
culturalTips.content: Chongqing Zoo is one of the best places in China to see giant pandas. The city is close to Sichuan's panda reserves. Pandas are native to the mountain bamboo forests of the region. Conservation efforts are strong locally.

culturalTips.title: Revolutionary Red Tourism
culturalTips.content: Chongqing's Red Cliff (红岩) area preserves revolutionary history. Zhazidong and Bai Gongguan museums commemorate revolutionary prisoners. The city was a key location during the Chinese Revolution and WWII.

culturalTips.title: Yangtze River Noodle Culture
culturalTips.content: Chongqing's '码头文化' (pier culture) influenced its cuisine. '重庆小面' (spicy noodles) was originally food for boatmen and laborers. The quick-cooking, heavily seasoned noodles remain the city's most popular breakfast.

culturalTips.title: Yi Niang Culture
culturalTips.content: 'Yi Niang' (幺令) is a distinctive Chongqing expression meaning 'one' and used as a general exclamation. The local dialect uses '儿化' (r-dropping) extensively. '老汉儿' (father) and '婆娘' (wife) are typical local terms.
